CIO added US-340b mid-sprint (connection_log state-change-dedup, ~99% row-volume reduction). Branched from sprint/sprint35-bugfixes-V0.27.9 per chain-end-merge rule.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.9 baseline. Ralph reports 285 tests pass cumulative no-regression run for Sprint 36; lint clean; red-green-revert-green TDD verified per fix per Ralph's `2026-05-14-from-ralph-v028-backlog-research-findings.md` PM inbox note.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Fix 3 P1 bugs surfaced by Drive 12 pharmacy-run 2026-05-13 plus one CIO mid-sprint dedup add.

CONTRACT GENERATION: This sprint.json was generated RETROACTIVELY 2026-05-14 from 3 inbox notes (offices/ralph/inbox/2026-05-13-from-marcus-us338/339/340-*.md) and Ralph's 2026-05-14 V0.27.10-ready handoff note. Original sprint ran in 'interactive mode' without a sprint.json on disk (Session 33 'one-off' decision; reverted by CIO Session 34).

RALPH SHIPPED 4 STORIES @ commit 6184a7f on sprint/sprint36-bugfixes-V0.27.10: US-338 (I-033 BT-reconnect fix direction B), US-339 (I-034 fd-leak fix via contextlib.closing), US-340 (I-035 HTTP-retry waste via hasRouteToServer() gate), US-340b (CIO mid-sprint add -- connection_log state-change-dedup).
IRL VALIDATION pending CIO drills post-deploy (4 gates per Ralph's PM note + Drive 12 retest + Drain 18+; see validation.bigDefinitionOfDone below).
OUTCOME branches per CIO 2026-05-14 directive: all green -> /sprint-validated + /chain-validated V0.27 chain to main + cut V0.28.0; any red -> V0.27.11 bug-fix sprint.

stories.title: I-033 BT no-reconnect after engine cycle -- heartbeat-fail spawns retry
stories.intent: Drive 12 pharmacy-run 2026-05-13 leg-2 lost because Pi software did not initiate a fresh connect_attempt on the second engine-on. Per Spool tech note (offices/ralph/inbox/2026-05-13-from-spool-bt-no-reconnect-after-engine-cycle.md), implement fix direction B: heartbeat-fail handler kicks a fresh _performConnect cycle on adapter-present-but-session-stale. Mode-agnostic (works in both car-fuse-box and wall-power-debug). connection_log records a reconnect_attempt event per cycle for auditability.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/connection_recovery.py (UPDATE -- _handleReconnectionFailure spawns daemon runReconnectHeartbeat with exponential backoff up to 15-min ceiling)
tests/test_orchestrator_connection_recovery_failure.py (UPDATE -- new TestUS338PostFailureHeartbeat class; all 8 tests green)
doNotTouch: AC-blip-trigger paths (false-fires in wall-power-debug; explicitly DEPRECATED fix direction A per Spool)

groundingRefs.value: Drive 12 leg-2 lost (connection_log silent for 30+ min post drive_end despite power_log AC-blips at engine-on)
groundingRefs.source: Spool 2026-05-13 PM note + Drive 12 forensic chat

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg _performConnect|_handleConnection|reconnect|heartbeat src/pi/obdii/ -- map current reconnect lifecycle
Fix: heartbeat-fail handler kicks fresh _performConnect cycle on stale-session detection; reconnect respects existing US-325 exponential backoff (V0.27.6)
DriveDetector re-arm: on successful reconnect, DriveDetector mints fresh drive_id on next engine-on
Observability: every connect_attempt writes a row to connection_log

Synthetic regression: paired BT + drive_start -> cut adapter power -> wait > 5s -> restore -> assert connect_attempt row in connection_log within 60s AND second drive_start within 90s. Test FAILS pre-fix.
invariants: Synthetic regression FAILS pre-fix (no reconnect attempt logged for 60s post drive_end)
I-025 / US-325 exponential backoff behavior preserved when adapter genuinely absent

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: See git diff at commit 6184a7f -- src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py + tests/pi/obdii/test_*.py
feedback.grounding: Spool tech note + Drive 12 forensic timeline + Ralph's 285-test cumulative no-regression run

stories.title: I-034 SQLite disk-I/O on long uptime -- fd-leak fix in pushDelta
stories.intent: After ~38 min uptime, long-lived eclipse-obd process threw sqlite3.OperationalError: disk I/O error on every sync_log read + realtime_data write. Database on disk fine (PRAGMA integrity_check ok). Root cause = file-descriptor leak from per-sweep sqlite3.connect() calls without explicit close. Fix: wrap with contextlib.closing in pushDelta + pushDriveCounter.

filesToTouch: src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE -- contextlib.closing wraps sqlite3.connect() in pushDelta + pushDriveCounter; the with-as gotcha that commits but does NOT close)
tests/pi/sync/test_sync_client.py (NEW -- synthetic harness asserts fd count stays flat across N sweeps)

groundingRefs.value: Drive 12 session: disk I/O error flood at 19:38 UTC (38 min after PID 1181 boot); error continued every heartbeat tick (5-10s) until service restart

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'sqlite3.connect|isolation_level|wal_checkpoint|OperationalError' src/pi/ -- map all DB connection lifecycles
Fix: contextlib.closing eliminates the ~13-fd-per-sweep leak in pushDelta + pushDriveCounter
Synthetic regression: N-sweep harness asserts fd count stays flat ~5-10 (not climbing); FAILS pre-fix
Production-validated test: harness runs equivalent of 6h+ idle soak in simulated time
invariants: Synthetic harness FAILS pre-fix (fd count climbs monotonically)
Sync correctness unchanged (no behavior change beyond resource cleanup)

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: See git diff at commit 6184a7f -- src/pi/sync/client.py + tests/pi/sync/test_*.py
feedback.grounding: Drive 12 journal flood + Ralph's 285-test cumulative no-regression run

stories.title: I-035 drive-time HTTP retry waste -- hasRouteToServer gate
stories.intent: Drive 12 session: ~84s of doomed TCP SYNs per ACTIVE-mode cadence tick when server unreachable. Plus CIO observed manual WiFi re-enable required post-drive (WiFi soft-off after server disconnect). Fix: SyncClient.hasRouteToServer() probe + orchestrator gate skips pushAllDeltas when no route. Reduces drive-time HTTP retry storms that may also trigger NetworkManager auto-disable behavior.

filesToTouch: src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE -- hasRouteToServer() method via socket probe)
tests/pi/orchestrator/test_sync_wiring.py (NEW -- synthetic regression for the no-route gate)

groundingRefs.value: Drive 12 session: ~84s of doomed TCP SYNs per ACTIVE-mode cadence tick during 8 min server-unreachable window

acceptance: Pre-flight: review Ralph commit 6184a7f for US-340 actually-touched files vs scope.filesToTouch above
hasRouteToServer() returns False when server unreachable (timeout < 1s); True when reachable
_maybeTriggerIntervalSync skips pushAllDeltas when hasRouteToServer() is False
Synthetic regression: mocked unreachable server -> assert pushAllDeltas not called
Production-equivalent test: 10-min drive simulation with server unreachable shows near-zero connection_log + sync_history rows
invariants: Synthetic regression FAILS pre-fix (pushAllDeltas called despite no route)
Recovery: once route restored, sync resumes within next heartbeat cycle

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: See git diff at commit 6184a7f -- src/pi/sync/client.py + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py + tests/pi/sync/test_*.py
feedback.grounding: Drive 12 journal flood + Ralph's 285-test cumulative no-regression run

stories.title: connection_log state-change-only dedup (CIO mid-sprint add)
stories.intent: CIO mid-sprint add 2026-05-14. connection_log was writing ~2000 rows/day during sustained adapter outages (one row per heartbeat retry). Dedup: only write a connection_log row when the connection STATE changes (not every retry tick). Drops sustained-outage volume to ~5-10 rows total.

filesToTouch: src/pi/data/connection_logger.py (NEW -- dedup connection_log writes by tracking last-state-emitted)
tests/pi/data/test_connection_log_event_types.py (NEW -- synthetic regression covers state-change dedup contract)
tests/pi/integration/test_bt_drop_resilience.py (UPDATE -- pre-existing US-211 assertion updated to match the dedup contract; called out inline per Ralph PM note)
doNotTouch: connection_log schema (no migration needed; just write fewer rows)

groundingRefs.value: ~2000 connection_log rows/day during sustained adapter outage pre-fix; ~5-10 rows total post-fix

acceptance: Pre-flight: review Ralph commit 6184a7f for US-340b actually-touched files vs scope.filesToTouch above
Dedup: connection_log row written only on state change (connect_attempt -> connect_failure -> connect_success) -- not per retry tick
Synthetic regression: 100 retry ticks during sustained outage emit < 10 connection_log rows total
US-211 integration test updated to match new dedup contract (called out inline per Ralph's PM note)
invariants: Pre-existing US-211 contract preserved EXCEPT for the dedup-related assertion (which is the intended change)
State transitions still always emit a row (never lose a state transition)

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: See git diff at commit 6184a7f -- src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py + tests/pi/obdii/test_*.py + tests/pi/integration/test_us_211_*.py

US-339 IRL: 6h+ bench soak post-deploy -> zero disk-I/O-error lines in journal; fd count for eclipse-obd PID stays flat ~5-10
US-340 IRL: 10-min drive post-deploy with server unreachable mid-drive -> server-side connection_log + sync_history row counts during drive near-zero
US-340b IRL: post-deploy bench soak with sustained adapter outage -> connection_log row volume ~5-10 total (not 2000)
Drive 12 retest (V0.27 chain gate): server drive_summary analytics fields populated within 30s of drive_end; Approach-1 drive_statistics rows for canonical PIDs
Drain 18+ (V0.27 chain gate): clean startup_log with prior_boot_clean=1 after V0.27.7 race-guard + V0.27.8 TZ + IO-class stress
